review_body: fair with cool undertones Don't get me wrong, this product is beautiful when first applied and it blends so nicely. It really does look like your skin is more airbrushed. However, I have combination skin..I've tried both applying an SPF, a good moisturizer and Urban Decay all nighter primer and then applying my foundation. And then I have also just applied my foundation on its own to see if there's a difference. After a few hours of wearing I can easily wipe off my foundation by just gently brushing a hair out of my face. I use Laura Mercier Translucent powder all over my face but it doesnt seem to make it set well. The area around my nose and around my forehead is super oily and cakey looking. I need to carry oil blotting papers and my beauty blender with a mini Translucent powder everywhere I go just to keep fixing my nose and forehead area. My coworker actually said she's experiencing the same thing and we've both decided to not use this foundation again. This is just a personal review, I honestly think this foundation might be great for others, as it is for my sister who LOVES it. However, this is my personal experience with it. Maybe see if you can try a sample first before buying Read more

foundation_names: Laura Mercier Tinted Blur Balm Skin  Tint
review_body: very Fair with neutral undertones I just wanted to share my experience with Laura Mercier’s Tinted Blur Balm because I was SO close to returning it for all the reasons listed here by others! The first time I applied it, it was later in the day and while I had applied skin care that morning, my dry skin was definitely not the most moisturized at that moment. What I saw literally had me put it back in the box to return! It clung to every single outline of my top skin layer even in parts of my face that I don’t typically have issues with. I honestly thought this was the worst product on the market. (I think LM should have offered more education into how to use this product, because without it, it seems like a dud!) However, the next day after properly doing my skin prep something in me decided to give it another try. This time I made sure to seal my skin with squalene (facial oil also works)- and it completely transformed the way it sat on my skin. I also tried various brushes for application, but find the best way is to just rub your finger in the pan and apply finger swatches all over the face, then blend those in with a softer foundation brush. (Don’t feel the need to “dig in”, just what is clinging to your finger with the warmth of your body and a normal amount of pressure.) My suspicion is for those who are going thru the product quickly, it’s because of these two issues- skin prep and application. The amount I’m using, if I were to apply this daily, would easily last me a couple months. However, the first time I tried it and had horrible texture issues, my skin was definitely sucking it up! I can imagine if you’re not giving it a super smooth base, you’ll definitely go thru it in no time and probably with less than stellar results. I think this is also why people with oily skin have been the skin type to most rave about this product, but I believe with these two adaptations anyone can use it successfully. Also, 00 was what Sephora recommended based on my in-store skin scan- I was a bit worrie Read more

The coverage is medium to full but still looks very natural on the skin. It does a great job covering redness and uneven tone without looking heavy or cakey. What I love most is the longevity. It lasts all day on me with very minimal touch-ups, even on busy days. It also doesn’t separate or settle into lines the way some long-wear foundations do. The shade 305N is a great neutral tone and blends really well with my skin. The formula is lightweight, easy to blend (I’ve used both a brush and sponge), and it gives a soft matte finish that still looks skin-like.
